atoms.content: A scalar is a single numerical value, representing only magnitude. In deep learning, loss (error) is almost always a scalar value.
A vector is a 1D array or list of numbers, representing magnitude and direction. It is a tensor of rank 1. A single row of features for one user is a vector.
A tensor is a generalized n-dimensional array. Scalar = 0D, vector = 1D, matrix = 2D. Higher dimensions are simply called tensors.
Think of a scalar as a dot, a vector as a line of dots, a matrix as a square of dots, and a 3D tensor as a cube of dots (like an RGB image).
If you are processing a batch of 32 color images, each 64x64 pixels, you need a 4D data structure. What is this structure called? (Answer: A 4D Tensor)
The norm measures a vector's length. The L2 norm (Euclidean distance) is used everywhere in ML — from regularization to measuring prediction error.
PyTorch uses torch.Tensor and TensorFlow uses tf.Tensor as their core data structure. All ML computations flow through tensors.
